# AC-02: GPAI & Foundation Model Obligation Screening

**Track:** AI Controls (`AC`) · **Priority:** SHOULD (recommended) · **Status:** draft

For changes that introduce or modify use of a general-purpose AI model (GPAI) or foundation model — including API-accessed LLMs (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face), multimodal models, and embedded AI services — screen for applicable obligations under EU AI Act Arts. 51-56. Classify the GPAI use as: Standard GPAI (normal use), Systemic Risk GPAI (trained with >10²⁵ FLOPs), or Prohibited High-Risk Use (real-time remote biometric ID, social scoring). Trigger corresponding compliance obligations and documentation requirements for downstream stages.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 1 | Intent Ingestion | Conditional | *Only applicable when the change involves a GPAI or foundation model. Screens for EU AI Act chapter III (GPAI) and chapter II (high-risk AI) obligations. Early screening prevents compliance surprises at design or deployment time.*

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Agent classifies, human validates |
| **Agent does** | Analyse the specification for any AI model use. If GPAI/foundation models are referenced: identify the model provider, model name, intended use, and training compute estimate. Classify as Standard, Systemic Risk, or Prohibited. Flag any obligations under Arts. 51-56 (transparency, documentation, adversarial testing). |
| **Human does** | Review agent classification. Confirm or override with documented justification. For Systemic Risk GPAIs, engage AI Ethics or Legal to confirm systemic risk status. Sign off on applicable obligation set before design begins. |

## Inputs

| Artifact | Source Control | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| Validated feature specification | QC-01 | Specification documenting AI model use case and intended purpose |


## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| GPAI Classification Decision | decision | Classification of GPAI as Standard, Systemic Risk, or Prohibited. List of applicable Arts. 51-56 obligations. Reference to GPAI provider's transparency disclosures. | 7 years (EU AI Act Art. 12 record-keeping) |
| Compliance Obligation Checklist | decision | Downstream controls triggered for Stages 2-6 (documentation, testing, monitoring) based on GPAI classification | 7 years |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | 120 min |
| **Tooling** | GPAI classification taxonomy (EU AI Act Chapter III), Model provenance tracker (Hugging Face Model Card, Model Card Meta, etc.) |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | escalate |
| **Escalate to** | AI Governance Lead |

If GPAI classification is unclear or contradictory, escalate to AI Governance Lead. Default to Systemic Risk classification pending resolution to ensure comprehensive compliance obligations are triggered.

## Dependencies

- `QC-01`
- `AC-01`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| EU AI Act | Art. 51 | Definition and scope of general-purpose AI models; triggers Chapter III obligations |
| EU AI Act | Art. 52 | Transparency obligations for GPAI: disclosure of training data, intended uses, known limitations, testing results |
| EU AI Act | Art. 53 | Technical documentation requirements for GPAI providers (model card, capabilities, testing results) |
| EU AI Act | Art. 55 | Systemic risk GPAIs (>10²⁵ FLOPs) must undergo additional adversarial testing, EU conformity assessment, and post-market monitoring |
| EU AI Act | Art. 56 | Provider obligations for systemic risk GPAIs including incident reporting |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** AI Governance · **Owner role:** AI Governance Lead
